posts.author: film
posts.body: Both Taner Trembley and Canon Swanson fought and won extremely impressively on LFA's last Louisville card. I was extremely impressed with both fighters during that card, interesting to see Trembley go from being one of the opening Prelims to now headlining a card. I really think he has potential, I'm around a **** ton of MMA and I can say that very few guys genuinely stand out and impress me to the point of thinking they could handle a UFC calibre opponent. Swanson is very green, but I really liked his game as well, look forward to this card massively.

posts.author: film
posts.body: Also, KillEmQuick is a banger ass nickname

posts.author: AyyLmaonnaise
posts.body: Predictions: 14 of 14 Pending | Tied for 1st with 598 others Trembley vs. JohnsTaner Trembley, SUB, R2I really hate trembley’s age but he seems ufc ready to me. Hes shown a deep bag of tricks through 6 fights, dominating everyone without breaking a sweat. His last win was a super impressive guillotine and the fight before that he was dominating until capping it off with a last second finish. Johns is just a worse version of his brother; low volume point fighter who’s an awful nail and not durable. Have to imagine dwcs or a short notice callup is next for Tanner after this; he turns 32 this year so it’s now or neverLozej vs. IbarraErnesto Ibarra, DECIbarra has auraTaylor vs. HoltDaniel Holt, TKO, R1Should be a highlight reel for Holt. I like him a lot. There are some very bad looks in his fights (dropped by and unable to finish Manno, his ammy career, etc.) but I do think the talent’s there. He’s got a long way to go but he’s only 22 and training at a top gym. He’s a huge LW and has a TKD background. His striking is crazy dynamic and he’s always throwing flashy kicks with little effort. He moves well and is very athletic, as well as having a huge frame. His grappling is very sus and he does get hit, but he is improving and you can see the talent. He’s also doing all the right things with his career: he’s fighting internationally and is getting different looks in camp. For example, he spent this camp at the PI in Mexico City, MTT, and is finishing up at Kill Clif rn. Taylor is an old guy and fights in combat night, one of the most fraudulent orgs in all of MMA. I expect Holt to smoke him, he is adamant about getting on dwcs so I imagine with a big win he is very closeSwanson vs. RichardsCanon Swanson, SUB, R1I always pick against Swanson and he ends up impressing every time. His long loss is really bad and the draw doesn’t seem great either but in all of his wins he’s wiping th floor with these guys. He should get a finish here, his opponents record is super paddedFau

posts.author: CangrejoRuiz
posts.body: Predictions: 13 of 14 Pending | Tied for 1st with 598 others Trembley vs. JohnsTaner Trembley, TKO, R3Trembley might be a bit old for a featherweight but he has been getting some solid wins in the LFA. He seems pretty well rounded. Elijah is nice step up in competition to really see his upside. Elijah himself is still a prospect for me but he seems to have hit his ceiling. He has shown more in the past than Trembley in the present so let's see if he can turn the dynamics around. I see both guys similarly talented right now so I am backing Trembley as he is raising his stock after every perfeomanceLozej vs. IbarraErnesto Ibarra, DECDevon was in a nice win streak before fighting Chapolin where he showed he is a decent regional challenge. He is a bit old but he hasn't shown any regression so far. Ibarra has the better prospect profile but he doesn't have any good wins. He is more of a grappler which is the best way to beat Lozej so I am leaning Ibarra. Lozej might be able to defend it and score more points in the scorecards so this will be competitive.Taylor vs. HoltDaniel Holt, TKO, R3Good fight between two guys with unanswered questions , Korey is a decent is a wrestler with limited striking skills while Holt is the young exciting up and comer with good striking skillset but a big TDD issue. The path for Taylor is clear as he should be able to land many takedowns but from what I have seen Holt's grappling defense is better than everyone Korey has faced so he might struggle to find the finish. Korey doesn't absorb damage too well so I can see Holt taking his chances on the feet and finishing the fight eventually.Swanson vs. RichardsCanon Swanson, TKO, R1I like Swanson even if he is kind of inconsistent but on a good day he is one of the more intriguing American lightweights on the circuit. Quintin is a good Grappler but his striking needs a lot of work and he struggles to get the takedowns against lesser competition. No need for Swanson to take this to the ground, I s

posts.author: Josh7
posts.body: Predictions: 14 of 14 Pending | Tied for 1st with 598 others Trembley vs. JohnsTaner Trembley, TKO, R1Johns is going to think too much about getting subbed and he will get TKOed.Lozej vs. IbarraErnesto Ibarra, TKO, R2Small age and reach advantage for Ibarra. Maybe a 2nd loss in a row by TKO for Lozej.Taylor vs. HoltDaniel Holt, TKO, R1Age advantage for Holt. Also undefeated.Swanson vs. RichardsQuintin Richards, TKO, R2This fight should be a fun as they both always win by TKO or submission. I will go with Richards that is undefeated but either one could win.Faust vs. CollinsSteve Collins, TKO, R1Collins by TKO. Undefeated.Melo vs. KulshinNikita Kulshin, DECKulshin is undefeated and this will stay that way.Harris vs. LatuRJ Harris, TKO, R1Age advantage and reach advantage. for Harri. Undefeated.Gasho vs. DjukanovicNoah Gasho, TKO, R1Age advantage for Gasho and undefeated.Sneve vs. KatambwaKelton Sneve, DECYou can consider Sneve undefeated as his only loss is a split decision. Age advantage as well for him.Jurado Sánchez vs. OryntaevYermakhan Oryntaev, DECJurado Sánchez has not fight got 17 months. I will go with Oryntaev by decision.Hernandez III vs. HuffRalph Hernandez III, TKO, R2This one could go either way but Huff has 2 split decision win that are debatable. I will go with Hernandez III by TKO.Nelson vs. MedranoErick Medrano, DECAge advantage for Medrano. Nelson is 37 years old. I'm going with the underdog Medrano.Hays vs. HartfieldKayara Hartfield, DECAge advantage for Hartfield.Bailey vs. AcostaSam Bailey, TKO, R1Acosta has not fight for 6 years. Age advantage for Bailey as well. * Edited at 04.15.2026, 10:23 AM ET *
